initial.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<example_1>
<example_grid_1>

7 7 7
0 7 7

7 7 7 7 7 7 7 7 7
0 7 7 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 7 7 7 7 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
</diagram>
</example_grid_1>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% Define sets:
row_nonzero(R) :- input(R, _, V), V != 0.
col_nonzero(C) :- input(_, C, V), V != 0.

% Count distinct rows and columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_nonzero(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_nonzero(C)}.

% The subgrid is comprised of these rows and columns.
% For each cell in subgrid we have a "tile". The output dimensions are N*N rows and M*M columns.
% We will generate output for each combination of tile row index i (0..N-1) and tile col index j (0..M-1), but we need to map i to actual rows.

% The mapping: For each tile (i_tile, j_tile) where i_tile in 0..N-1, j_tile in 0..M-1, and for each subgrid cell (dr, dc) where dr in subgrid rows and dc in subgrid cols (dr and dc are actual row/col indices that are in the subgrid), define absolute position R = i_tile * N + (dr_index)?? Similarly for column c, relative index idx_c = #count{C2 : col_nonzero(C2), C2 < c}. This can be defined using #count and comparison.

```
% Define non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Core cells: non-zero cells that have a non-zero orthogonal neighbor
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R + DR, C2 = C + DC.
core(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V),
               (exists R2,C2,V2 : input(R2,C2,V2), nonzero(R2,C2,V2),
                              (R2 = R + 0, C2 = C + 1) ; (R2 = R + 0, C2 = C - 1;
                                 R2 = R + 1, C2 = C; R2 = R - 1, C2 = C) ).

% Define rows and columns that contain core cells
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Count distinct core rows and columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% Define index of core rows/cols (0-based)
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Output cell mapping: for each core cell (BR,BC,V) and each core cell (DR,DC,V2)
output_cell(R_abs, C_abs) :-
    core(BR, BC, V),
    core(DR, DC, V2),
    n_rows(N),
    n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I),
    col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2),
    col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one color for each output cell
1 { output(R_abs, C_abs, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs, C_abs).

% Non-zero block rule: if core(BR,BC,V) and V != 0 then the block copies subgrid values
:- core(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   core(DR, DC, V2),
   n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
   row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
   row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
   R_abs = I * N + I2,
   C_abs = J * M + J2,
   output(R_abs, C_abs, V2).

% Zero block rule: if core(BR,BC,0) then the whole block is zero
:- core(BR, BC, 0),
   core(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
   row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
   row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
   R_abs = I * N + I2,
   C_abs = J * M + J2,
   output(R_abs, C_abs, 0).
```

```
% Core non-zero cells with a non-zero neighbor
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R + DR, C2 = C + DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- input(R+0, C+1, V), V != 0.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- input(R+0, C-1, V), V != 0.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- input(R+1, C, V), V != 0.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- input(R-1, C, V), V != 0.

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Count distinct core rows and columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% Index of core rows and columns (0‑based)
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Subgrid cell (contains zeros as well)
subcell(R,C,V) :- row_core(R), col_core(C), input(R,C,V).

% Mapping to output cells
output_cell(R_abs, C_abs) :-
    core(BR,BC,V_anchor),
    subcell(DR,DC,V2),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR,I), col_index_of(BC,J),
    row_index_of(DR,I2), col_index_of(DC,J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one color for each output cell
1 { output(R_abs, C_abs, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs, C_abs).

% Non‑zero anchor block copies the whole subgrid
:- core(BR,BC,V_anchor), V_anchor != 0,
    subcell(DR,DC,V2),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR,I), col_index_of(BC,J),
    row_index_of(DR,I2), col_index_of(DC,J2),
```

% Core non‑zero cells that have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R + DR, C2 = C + DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C+1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C-1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+1, C, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R-1, C, V), V != 0).

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows and columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows and columns (size of the tile)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% 0‑based index of a core row / column
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Sub‑grid cell (includes zeros – we keep the whole rectangle)
subcell(R,C,V) :- row_core(R), col_core(C), input(R,C,V).

% Output cell – map each tile (BR,BC) and each sub‑grid cell (DR,DC) to an absolute position
output_cell(R_abs, C_abs) :-
    core(BR, BC, _),               % anchor cell of the tile
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),           % value of the copied cell
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one colour for every output cell
1 { output(R_abs, C_abs, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs, C_abs).

% Non‑zero anchor tile – copy the whole sub‑grid into the tile
:- core(BR, BC, V_anchor), V_anchor != 0,
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, V2).

% Zero anchor tile – the whole tile must be all zero
:- core(BR, BC, 0),
    subcell(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, 0).

full_program:
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Core cells: a non‑zero cell that has at least one orthogonal neighbour.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :-
    (R2 = R+1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R-1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (C2 = C+1, input(R,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (C2 = C-1, input(R,C2,V2), V2 != 0).

core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Rows / columns that contain a core cell.
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of core rows / columns (size of the square tile)
core_h = #count{R : row_core(R)}.      % height
core_w = #count{C : col_core(C)}.      % width

% Bounding rectangle of the core cells.
core_min_row = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_max_row = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_min_col = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_max_col = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Whole grid limits.
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Anchor for the copy (right side if square, else left side).
anchor_r = core_max_row + core_h - 1.
% right‑side copy is used when the tile fits (and does not overlap core),
% otherwise we fall back to the left side (column 0).
anchor_c_right = core_max_col + core_w - 1.
anchor_c_left  = 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Core tile values – the same values are used for the original placement
% and for the copy.  (dr,dc) are the coordinates inside the square tile.
core_tile_value(I,J,V) :-
    I >= 0, I < core_h,
    J >= 0, J < core_w,
    input(core_min_row + I, core_min_col + J, V).

% Original core tile (at its original location).
core_original(R,C,V) :-
    R = core_min_row + I,
    C = core_min_col + J,
    I >= 0, I < core_h,
    J >= 0, J < core_w,
    core_tile_value(I,J,V).

% Copy of the core tile.
% Right‑side copy (uses column anchor_c_right when it fits).
copy_tile_right(R,C,V) :-
    R = anchor_r + I,
    C = anchor_c_right + J,
    I >= 0, I < core_h,
    J >= 0, J < core_w,
    core_tile_value(I,J,V),
    J <= I,
    not core_original(R,C,_).          % do not overlap the original core

% Left‑side copy (always fits, placed at column 0).
copy_tile_left(R,C,V) :-
    R = anchor_r + I,
    C = anchor_c_left + J,
    I >= 0, I < core_h,
    J >= 0, J < core_w,
    core_tile_value(I,J,V),
    J <= I,
    not core_original(R,C,_).

% The copy tile – either side may be valid; the solver will pick the one that
% satisfies all constraints (right side if it fits, otherwise left side).
copy_tile(R,C,V) :- copy_tile_right(R,C,V).
copy_tile(R,C,V) :- copy_tile_left(R,C,V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Output specification
output(R,C,V) :- core_original(R,C,V).
output(R,C,V) :- copy_tile(R,C,V).
output(R,C,0) :- not core_original(R,C,_), not copy_tile(R,C,_), input(R,C,_), V = 0.

% Exactly one colour for every cell.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output(R,C,Color).

Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% Core non‑zero cells that have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R + DR, C2 = C + DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C+1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C-1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+1, C, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R-1, C, V), V != 0).

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows and columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows and columns (size of the tile)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% 0‑based index of a core row / column
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Sub‑grid cell (includes zeros – we keep the whole rectangle)
subcell(R,C,V) :- row_core(R), col_core(C), input(R,C,V).

% Output cell – map each tile (BR,BC) and each sub‑grid cell (DR,DC) to an absolute position
output_cell(R_abs, C_abs) :-
    core(BR, BC, _),               % anchor cell of the tile
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),           % value of the copied cell
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one colour for every output cell
1 { output(R_abs, C_abs, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs, C_abs).

% Non‑zero anchor tile – copy the whole sub‑grid into the tile
:- core(BR, BC, V_anchor), V_anchor != 0,
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, V2).

% Zero anchor tile – the whole tile must be all zero
:- core(BR, BC, 0),
    subcell(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, 0).

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

% Define delta
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% has non-zero neighbor
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- neighbor(R,C,R2,C2), input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% core cells: non-zero with non-zero neighbor
core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Determine distinct core rows and columns
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% Row index of core row
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Subcell: core cell value
subcell(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V).

% Output cell positions for each anchor core cell and each subcell
output_cell(R_abs,C_abs) :-
    core(BR,BC,_),        % anchor core cell of tile
    subcell(DR,DC,V2),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR,I), col_index_of(BC,J),
    row_index_of(DR,I2), col_index_of(DC,J2),
    R_abs = I * n_rows(N) + I2,   % note n_rows(N) is count of core rows
    C_abs = J * n_cols(M) + J2.

% Exactly one color per output cell
1 { output(R_abs,C_abs,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs,C_abs).

% Additionally, for anchor cells that are zero (i.e., not core), we need to output zero blocks.
% We'll treat any input cell that is not core as zero anchor.
zero_anchor(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not core(R,C,_).

% zero tile output
zero_output_cell(R_abs,C_abs) :-
    zero_anchor(BR,BC),
    subcell(DR,DC,_),    % we can reuse subcell (any core row/col) - but values not needed
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR,I), col_index_of(BC,J),
    row_index_of(DR,I2), col_index_of(DC,J2),
    R_abs = I * n_rows(N) + I2,
    C_abs = J * n_cols(M) + J2.

% Output cells from zero anchors should be 0
1 { output(R_abs,C_abs,Color) : color(0) } 1 :- zero_output_cell(R_abs,C_abs).

% Ensure that positions from core tiles and zero tiles do not conflict (should be disjoint). If they overlap, we need to prioritize core (maybe core overrides). We can use preference: core tiles have higher priority. We can set rule ordering: core tiles use output(R_abs,C_abs,Color) with Color != 0 constraint; zero tiles assign Color = 0. The core tiles will assign Color maybe non-zero; zero tiles assign 0; overlapping positions may cause conflict but hopefully not happen.

delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- neighbor(R,C,R2,C2), input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Subcell is a core cell
subcell(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V).

% Output cell mapping: each tile anchored at core cell (BR,BC) copies subcell (DR,DC,V2)

% Exactly one color per output cell
1 { output(R_abs,C_abs,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs,C_abs).

% Zero tile: if anchor is zero, produce zero block
zero_anchor(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not core(R,C,_).

    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs,C_abs,0).

% Ensure zero tile outputs for positions not covered by core tiles:
output(R_abs,C_abs,Color) :-
    zero_output_cell(R_abs,C_abs),
    Color = 0.

The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). <asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% Core non‑zero cells that have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R + DR, C2 = C + DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C+1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C-1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+1, C, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R-1, C, V), V != 0).

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows and columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows and columns (size of the tile)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% 0‑based index of a core row / column
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Sub‑grid cell (includes zeros – we keep the whole rectangle)
subcell(R,C,V) :- row_core(R), col_core(C), input(R,C,V).

% Output cell – map each tile (BR,BC) and each sub‑grid cell (DR,DC) to an absolute position
output_cell(R_abs, C_abs) :-
    core(BR, BC, _),               % anchor cell of the tile
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),           % value of the copied cell
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one colour for every output cell
1 { output(R_abs, C_abs, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs, C_abs).

% Non‑zero anchor tile – copy the whole sub‑grid into the tile
:- core(BR, BC, V_anchor), V_anchor != 0,
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, V2).

% Zero anchor tile – the whole tile must be all zero
:- core(BR, BC, 0),
    subcell(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, 0).

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

% Determine core cells:
core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, hasNonZeroNeighbor(R,C).

hasNonZeroNeighbor(R,C) :- input(R+DR, C+DC, V2), V2 != 0 for some neighbor (dr,dc) among four directions.

% Determine core bounding box:
min_core_row(R) :- core(R,_,_), not exists R2 < R with core(R2,_,_). (But we can compute using #min.)

% Use #min and #max.

% Define core tile values: coreTile(DR,DC,TileColor) :- coreTileRow = min_core_row + DR, coreTileCol = min_core_col + DC, input(coreTileRow, coreTileCol, TileColor).

% Determine anchor zero cells: anchor(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R > max_core_row.

% Determine copy placement:

Attempt_2 code:

% Core non‑zero cells that have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- neighbor(R,C,R2,C2),
                            input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows / columns that contain at least one core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows / columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% Index of a core row / column (0‑based)
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Sub‑grid cell – the values of the core cells (the “tile”)
subcell(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V).

% Position of a copied cell (tile placement)
output_cell(R_abs,C_abs) :-
    core(BR, BC, _),               % anchor cell of the tile
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),           % value of the copied cell
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR,I), col_index_of(BC,J),
    row_index_of(DR,I2), col_index_of(DC,J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one colour for every output cell
1 { output(R_abs, C_abs, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs, C_abs).

% Non‑zero anchor tile – copy the whole sub‑grid into the tile
:- core(BR, BC, V_anchor), V_anchor != 0,
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, V2).

% Zero anchor tile – the whole tile must be all zero
:- core(BR, BC, 0),
    subcell(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, 0).

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

```
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% A cell has a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0,
    (R2 = R+1, C2 = C) ; (R2 = R-1, C2 = C) ; (R2 = R, C2 = C+1) ; (R2 = R, C2 = C-1).

% Core cells: non‑zero and have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2).

% Rows and columns containing core cells
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Size of the core tile
N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% Index of a core row / column (0‑based)
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Minimal row and column of the core tile
min_core_row = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
min_core_col = #min{C : col_core(C)}.

% Sub‑grid cell (the core tile)
subcell(DR,DC,V) :- core(min_core_row+DR, min_core_col+DC, V).

% Copy of the core tile anchored at a zero cell that lies below the core tile
anchor(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R > max_core_row.

max_core_row = #max{R : row_core(R)}.

copy(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    anchor(AR,AC),
    subcell(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = AR + DR,
    Cabs = AC + DC.

% The core tile at its original position (must stay unchanged)
core_tile(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    subcell(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = min_core_row + DR,
    Cabs = min_core_col + DC.

% Cells that are non‑zero but not part of the core become 0
non_core_nonzero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, not core(R,C,V).

% For each cell we state the correct colour.

    core_tile(R,C,V);
output(R,C,V) :-
    copy(R,C,V);
output(R,C,0) :-
    non_core_nonzero(R,C), V = 0.

% The grid has exactly the same shape as the input

% Exactly one colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
```

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% Core non‑zero cells that have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R + DR, C2 = C + DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C+1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C-1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+1, C, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R-1, C, V), V != 0).

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows and columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows and columns (size of the tile)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% 0‑based index of a core row / column
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Sub‑grid cell (includes zeros – we keep the whole rectangle)
subcell(R,C,V) :- row_core(R), col_core(C), input(R,C,V).

% Output cell – map each tile (BR,BC) and each sub‑grid cell (DR,DC) to an absolute position
output_cell(R_abs, C_abs) :-
    core(BR, BC, _),               % anchor cell of the tile
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),           % value of the copied cell
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one colour for every output cell
1 { output(R_abs, C_abs, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs, C_abs).

% Non‑zero anchor tile – copy the whole sub‑grid into the tile

    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, V2).

% Zero anchor tile – the whole tile must be all zero

    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,

    output(R_abs, C_abs, 0).

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

<asp>
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Core cells – a non‑zero cell that has at least one orthogonal neighbour
% which is also non‑zero.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0,
    (R2 = R+1, C2 = C) ; (R2 = R-1, C2 = C) ; (R2 = R, C2 = C+1) ; (R2 = R, C2 = C-1).

core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Rows and columns that contain at least one core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Size of the core tile (number of distinct core rows / columns)
N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.      % height of the core tile
M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.      % width  of the core tile

% Minimal core row / column (used as the top‑left corner of the tile)
min_core_row = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
min_core_col = #min{C : col_core(C)}.

% Index of a core row / column (0‑based)
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% The core tile – the sub‑grid formed by the core cells.
subcell(DR,DC,V) :-
    core(min_core_row+DR, min_core_col+DC, V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Zero cells that lie strictly below the core tile become copy anchors.
max_core_row = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
anchor(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,0), R > max_core_row.

% Copy the core tile anchored at each zero anchor.
copy(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    anchor(AR,AC),
    subcell(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = AR + DR,
    Cabs = AC + DC.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% The core tile at its original position must stay unchanged.
core_tile(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    subcell(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = min_core_row + DR,
    Cabs = min_core_col + DC.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells that are non‑zero but are not part of the core become 0.
non_core_nonzero(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    not core(R,C,V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% For every grid cell we state the correct colour.
output(R,C,V) :-
    core_tile(R,C,V);
output(R,C,V) :-
    copy(R,C,V);
output(R,C,0) :-
    non_core_nonzero(R,C), V = 0.

% The output grid has exactly the same shape as the input.
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour for each cell.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

Define core_h = #count{R : core(R,_,_)}.

```asp
% neighbor offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Core cells: non‑zero cells that have at least one orthogonal non‑zero neighbour
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2).

% rows / cols that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% bounds of core rectangle
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% dimensions of the core tile (assume core rows/cols are contiguous)
core_h = #count{R : core(R,_,_) }.      % number of core rows
core_w = #count{C : core(_,C,_)}.       % number of core columns

% maximum input coordinates (0‑based)
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% convenience bounds for a tile of size core_h × core_w
max_row_plus_one = max_row + 1.
max_col_plus_one = max_col + 1.

% non‑zero cells anywhere (used for block checks)
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% check whether the core tile placed at (R0,C0) contains a non‑zero cell
block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V) :-
    nonzero(R,C,V),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w.

block_has_nonzero(R0,C0) :- block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V).

% a zero cell can be a tile anchor iff it is all‑zero and big enough
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h <= max_row_plus_one,
    C0 + core_w <= max_col_plus_one,
    not block_has_nonzero(R0,C0).

% cells that belong to the core rectangle
core_rect(R,C) :- core(R,C,_), R >= core_row_min, R <= core_row_max, C >= core_col_min, C <= core_col_max.

% value of a core cell (used for the tile)
core_set(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V), core_rect(R,C).

% copy of the core tile – for each copy‑anchor cell we copy the core values
copy_output(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(R0+dr, C0+dc, V).

copy_rect(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).

% set of cells that belong to the copy tile (non‑zero values)
copy_rect_set(R,C,V) :- copy_rect(R,C,V).

% a set for zero cells that are not part of the core rectangle nor the copy tile
zero_set(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), not core_rect(R,C), not copy_rect_set(R,C,_).

% output cell – any grid position
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per cell – we give a colour for each of the three disjoint sets
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- core_set(R,C,V), Color = V, output_cell(R,C).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- copy_rect(R,C,V), Color = V, output_cell(R,C).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- zero_set(R,C), Color = 0, output_cell(R,C).
```

```
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% neighbor check
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% core cells: non-zero with at least one orthogonal non-zero neighbour
core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% rows/cols that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% bounds of core rectangle
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% dimensions of the core tile (assume core rows/cols contiguous)
core_h = #count{R : core(R,_,_) }.
core_w = #count{C : core(_,C,_)}.

% grid dimensions
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

max_row_plus_one = max_row + 1.
max_col_plus_one = max_col + 1.

% check for non-zero cell in a block
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V) :-
    nonzero(R,C,V),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w.

block_has_nonzero(R0,C0) :- block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V).

% zero anchor: zero cell with an all-zero core‑size block
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h <= max_row_plus_one,
    C0 + core_w <= max_col_plus_one,
    not block_has_nonzero(R0,C0).

% core rectangle cells
core_rect(R,C) :- core(R,C,_), R >= core_row_min, R <= core_row_max, C >= core_col_min, C <= core_col_max.

% value of a core cell (used for the tile)
% (already available via core(R,C,V))

% copy of the core tile onto a zero anchor
copy_output(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(Rmin+dr, Cmin+dc, V).   % core tile value at offset (dr,dc)

copy_output_flag(R,C) :- copy_output(R,C,V).

% zero cells that are not part of the core rectangle nor the copy tile
zero_set(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output_flag(R,C).

% guards for the three mutually exclusive sets
core_guard(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V), core_rect(R,C).

copy_guard(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).

zero_guard(R,C) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output_flag(R,C), input(R,C,_).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per cell – we have three disjoint sets
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- core_guard(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- copy_guard(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- zero_guard(R,C), Color = 0.
```

```asp
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% A non‑zero cell that has a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% Core cells: non‑zero and have a non‑zero neighbour
core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R).

% Rows / columns that contain at least one core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Bounding rectangle of the core cells
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Height and width of the core tile (assume contiguous)
core_h = #count{R : core(R,_,_)}.
core_w = #count{C : core(_,C,_)}.

% Grid bounds (0‑based)
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

max_row_plus_one = max_row + 1.
max_col_plus_one = max_col + 1.

% Helper: any non‑zero cell
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Does the core‑size block placed at (R0,C0) contain a non‑zero cell?
block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V) :-
    nonzero(R,C,V),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w.

block_has_nonzero(R0,C0) :- block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V).

% A zero cell can be a tile anchor iff the whole core tile can be placed there without touching a non‑zero cell
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h <= max_row_plus_one,
    C0 + core_w <= max_col_plus_one,
    not block_has_nonzero(R0,C0).

% Core rectangle (the sub‑grid that contains the core cells)
core_rect(R,C) :- core(R,C,_), R >= core_row_min, R <= core_row_max, C >= core_col_min, C <= core_col_max.

% Copy of the core tile onto a zero anchor
copy_output(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(core_row_min + dr, core_col_min + dc, V).

copy_output_flag(R,C) :- copy_output(R,C,V).

% Cells that are not in the core rectangle nor in the copy tile
zero_set(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output_flag(R,C).

% Guard predicates for the three disjoint parts
core_guard(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V), core_rect(R,C).
copy_guard(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).
zero_guard(R,C) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output_flag(R,C), input(R,C,_).

% Output cell (every grid position)
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour for each cell – three exclusive groups
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- core_guard(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- copy_guard(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- zero_guard(R,C), Color = 0.
```

```asp
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Neighbor with non‑zero value
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% Core cells: non‑zero and have a non‑zero neighbour
core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows/cols containing a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Bounding rectangle of core cells
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Tile dimensions (contiguous rows/cols)
core_h = #count{R : core(R,_,_)}.
core_w = #count{C : core(_,C,_)}.

% Grid size
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

max_row_plus_one = max_row + 1.
max_col_plus_one = max_col + 1.

% Non‑zero cells (anywhere)
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Does the core‑size block placed at (R0,C0) contain a non‑zero cell?
block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V) :-
    nonzero(R,C,V),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w.

block_has_nonzero(R0,C0) :- block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V).

% Zero anchor: a zero cell where the whole tile can be placed without touching a non‑zero cell
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h <= max_row_plus_one,
    C0 + core_w <= max_col_plus_one,
    not block_has_nonzero(R0,C0).

% Core rectangle (the sub‑grid that contains the core cells)
core_rect(R,C) :- core(R,C,_), R >= core_row_min, R <= core_row_max, C >= core_col_min, C <= core_col_max.

% Copy of the core tile onto a zero anchor
copy_output(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(core_row_min + dr, core_col_min + dc, V).

% Flag that indicates a copy‑cell (non‑zero)
copy_output_flag(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).

% Cells not belonging to the core rectangle nor to the copy tile
default_guard(R,C) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output_flag(R,C,_), input(R,C,_).

% Guard predicates
core_guard(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V), core_rect(R,C).
copy_guard(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).
zero_guard(R,C) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output_flag(R,C,_), input(R,C,_).

% Output predicates for each part
output_cell_core(R,C,V) :- core_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_copy(R,C,V) :- copy_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_zero(R,C) :- zero_guard(R,C).

% Exactly one colour for each cell – three disjoint sets
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_core(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_copy(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_zero(R,C), Color = 0.
```

```asp
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% neighbor with non-zero
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% Core cells: non‑zero and have a non‑zero neighbour
core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% rows/cols containing a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Bounding rectangle of core cells
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Tile dimensions (contiguous rows/cols)
core_h = #count{R : core(R,_,_)}.
core_w = #count{C : core(_,C,_)}.

% Grid bounds (0‑based)
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.
max_row_plus_one = max_row + 1.
max_col_plus_one = max_col + 1.

% any non‑zero cell
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Does the core‑size block placed at (R0,C0) contain a non‑zero cell?
block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V) :-
    nonzero(R,C,V),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w.

block_has_nonzero(R0,C0) :- block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V).

% Zero anchor: a zero cell that can host the tile
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h <= max_row_plus_one,
    C0 + core_w <= max_col_plus_one,
    not block_has_nonzero(R0,C0).

% Core rectangle (the sub‑grid that contains the core cells)
core_rect(R,C) :- core(R,C,_), R >= core_row_min, R <= core_row_max, C >= core_col_min, C <= core_col_max.

% Copy of the core tile onto a zero anchor
copy_output(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(core_row_min + dr, core_col_min + dc, V).

% Flag for copy cells
copy_output_flag(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).

% Cells not in core rectangle nor copy tile
zero_guard(R,C) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output_flag(R,C,_), input(R,C,_).

% Guard predicates
core_guard(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V), core_rect(R,C).
copy_guard(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).
zero_guard(R,C) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output_flag(R,C,_), input(R,C,_).

% Output predicates for each guard
output_cell_core(R,C,V) :- core_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_copy(R,C,V) :- copy_guard(R,C,V).
```

```asp
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% A non‑zero cell that has a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% Core cells: non‑zero and have at least one orthogonal non‑zero neighbour
core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R).

% Rows / columns containing core cells
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Bounding rectangle of core cells
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Dimensions of the core tile (contiguous rows/cols)
core_h = #count{R : core(R,_,_)}.
core_w = #count{C : core(_,C,_)}.

% Grid bounds (0‑based)
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.
max_row_plus_one = max_row + 1.
max_col_plus_one = max_col + 1.

% Any non‑zero cell
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Does the core‑size block placed at (R0,C0) contain a non‑zero cell?
block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V) :-
    nonzero(R,C,V),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w.

block_has_nonzero(R0,C0) :- block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V).

% Zero anchor: a zero cell where the core tile can be placed without touching a non‑zero cell
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h <= max_row_plus_one,
    C0 + core_w <= max_col_plus_one,
    not block_has_nonzero(R0,C0).

% Core rectangle (the sub‑grid that contains the core cells)
core_rect(R,C) :- core(R,C,_), R >= core_row_min, R <= core_row_max, C >= core_col_min, C <= core_col_max.

% Copy of the core tile onto a zero anchor
copy_output(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(core_row_min + dr, core_col_min + dc, V).

% Guard predicates
core_guard(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V), core_rect(R,C).
copy_guard(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).
zero_guard(R,C) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output(R,C,_), input(R,C,_).

% Output predicates for each part
output_cell_core(R,C,V) :- core_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_copy(R,C,V) :- copy_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_zero(R,C) :- zero_guard(R,C).

% Exactly one colour per cell – three exclusive groups
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_core(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_copy(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_zero(R,C), Color = 0.
```

copy_guard(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).
zero_guard(R,C,V) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output(R,C,V), input(R,C,_).

% output predicates for each part
output_cell_core(R,C,V) :- core_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_copy(R,C,V) :- copy_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_zero(R,C,V) :- zero_guard(R,C,V).

% exactly one colour for every cell – three exclusive sets
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_core(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_copy(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_zero(R,C,V), Color = 0.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% Core non‑zero cells that have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R + DR, C2 = C + DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C+1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C-1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+1, C, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R-1, C, V), V != 0).

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows and columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows and columns (size of the tile)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% 0‑based index of a core row / column
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Sub‑grid cell (includes zeros – we keep the whole rectangle)
subcell(R,C,V) :- row_core(R), col_core(C), input(R,C,V).

% Output cell – map each tile (BR,BC) and each sub‑grid cell (DR,DC) to an absolute position
output_cell(R_abs, C_abs) :-
    core(BR, BC, _),               % anchor cell of the tile
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),           % value of the copied cell
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one colour for every output cell
1 { output(R_abs, C_abs, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs, C_abs).

% Non‑zero anchor tile – copy the whole sub‑grid into the tile
:- core(BR, BC, V_anchor), V_anchor != 0,
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, V2).

% Zero anchor tile – the whole tile must be all zero
:- core(BR, BC, 0),
    subcell(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Offsets for 4‑connected neighbourhood
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% A cell has at least one orthogonal neighbour with a non‑zero value
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- neighbor(R,C,R2,C2),
                            input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% Core cells: non‑zero and have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows / columns that contain at least one core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows / columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% Index of a core row / column (0‑based)
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Sub‑grid cell – the values of the core cells (the “tile”)
subcell(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V).

% Position of a copied cell (tile placement)
output_cell(R_abs,C_abs) :-
    core(BR,BC,_),               % anchor cell of the tile
    subcell(DR,DC,V2),           % value of the copied cell
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR,I), col_index_of(BC,J),
    row_index_of(DR,I2), col_index_of(DC,J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one colour for every output cell
1 { output(R_abs,C_abs,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs,C_abs).
</asp>

<asp>
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Core cells – a non‑zero cell that has at least one orthogonal neighbour
% which is also non‑zero.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0,
    (R2 = R+1, C2 = C) ; (R2 = R-1, C2 = C) ; (R2 = R, C2 = C+1) ; (R2 = R, C2 = C-1).

core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Rows and columns that contain at least one core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Size of the core tile (number of distinct core rows / columns)
N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.      % height of the core tile
M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.      % width  of the core tile

% Minimal core row / column (used as the top‑left corner of the tile)
min_core_row = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
min_core_col = #min{C : col_core(C)}.

% Index of a core row / column (0‑based)
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% The core tile – the sub‑grid formed by the core cells.
subcell(DR,DC,V) :-
    core(min_core_row+DR, min_core_col+DC, V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Zero cells that lie strictly below the core tile become copy anchors.
max_core_row = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
anchor(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,0), R > max_core_row.

% Copy the core tile anchored at each zero anchor.
copy(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    anchor(AR,AC),
    subcell(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = AR + DR,
    Cabs = AC + DC.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% The core tile at its original position must stay unchanged.
core_tile(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    subcell(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = min_core_row + DR,
    Cabs = min_core_col + DC.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells that are non‑zero but are not part of the core become 0.
non_core_nonzero(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    not core(R,C,V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% For every grid cell we state the correct colour.
output(R,C,V) :-
    core_tile(R,C,V);
output(R,C,V) :-
    copy(R,C,V);
output(R,C,0) :-
    non_core_nonzero(R,C), V = 0.

% The output grid has exactly the same shape as the input.
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour for each cell.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

<asp>
% ---------- neighbours ----------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% a non‑zero cell has a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% core cells: non‑zero and have a non‑zero neighbour
core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R).

% rows / columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% bounding rectangle of the core cells
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% dimensions of the core tile (contiguous rows/cols)
core_h = #count{R : core(R,_,_)}.
core_w = #count{C : core(_,C,_)}.

% grid size (0‑based)
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.
max_row_plus_one = max_row + 1.
max_col_plus_one = max_col + 1.

% any non‑zero cell
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% does the core‑size block placed at (R0,C0) contain a non‑zero cell?
block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V) :-
    nonzero(R,C,V),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w.

block_has_nonzero(R0,C0) :- block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V).

% zero anchor: a zero cell where the whole tile can be placed without touching a non‑zero cell
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h <= max_row_plus_one,
    C0 + core_w <= max_col_plus_one,
    not block_has_nonzero(R0,C0).

% core rectangle (the sub‑grid that contains the core cells)
core_rect(R,C) :- core(R,C,_), R >= core_row_min, R <= core_row_max, C >= core_col_min, C <= core_col_max.

% copy of the core tile onto a zero anchor
copy_output(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(core_row_min + dr, core_col_min + dc, V).

% guard predicates for the three disjoint parts
core_guard(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V), core_rect(R,C).
copy_guard(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).
zero_guard(R,C,V) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output(R,C,V), input(R,C,_).

% output predicates for each part
output_cell_core(R,C,V) :- core_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_copy(R,C,V) :- copy_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_zero(R,C,V) :- zero_guard(R,C,V).

% exactly one colour for every cell – three exclusive sets
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_core(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_copy(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_zero(R,C,V), Color = 0.
</asp>

The output consists of exactly two copies of this tile: one placed at the original top-left corner of the core tile (core_row_min, core_col_min) and another placed at the lexicographically smallest zero cell (R,C) such that R >= core_row_min + core_h, C >= core_col_min (or some condition) and the tile does not overlap core cells. This will produce the correct outputs.

Implementation: Compute core_tile anchor (original) as (core_row_min, core_col_min). Copy tile there.

Compute zero_anchor candidate: zero_anchor(R0,C0) :- input(R0,C0,0), R0 >= core_row_min, R0 + core_h -1 <= max_row, C0 >= core_col_min, C0 + core_w -1 <= max_col, not conflict(R0,C0), not (R0 = core_row_min, C0 = core_col_min). (excludes original anchor). Then define zero_anchor_selected(R0,C0) :- zero_anchor_candidate(R0,C0), not exists zero_anchor_candidate(R1,C1) where (R1 < R0) or (R1 = R0 and C1 < C0). This selects lexicographically smallest candidate.

Then copy tile at zero_anchor_selected.

Now we have exactly two copies: original + selected zero anchor.

Now we also need to output tile values for each copy.

Implementation details:

Define core_tile_value(DR,DC,V) :- core_cell(RC,CC,V), DR = RC - core_row_min, DC = CC - core_col_min.

Now for original copy: For each core_tile_value(DR,DC,V), output (core_row_min+DR, core_col_min+DC, V). This is basically same as copying tile at core anchor.

Define copy at zero anchor: For each core_tile_value(DR,DC,V), output (R0+DR, C0+DC, V).

Finally, for any cell not covered by any copy (i.e., output cell not set), we set output zero.

We need to ensure that for each copy we don't produce duplicate outputs for same cell with conflicting values. For core anchor copy and zero anchor copy may produce overlapping cells only if zero_anchor = core_anchor (but we exclude that). So safe.

Define:

% Offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-zero neighbor
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :- 
    neighbor(R,C,R2,C2), 
    input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0.

neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R + DR, C2 = C + DC.

% Core cell: non-zero and has non-zero neighbor
core_cell(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

% Row and column containing a core cell
row_core(R) :- core_cell(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core_cell(_,C,_).

% Determine core bounding rectangle
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Tile dimensions
core_h = core_row_max - core_row_min + 1.
core_w = core_col_max - core_col_min + 1.

% Max indices
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Conflict predicate: tile would overlap a core cell with a non-zero value.
conflict(R0,C0) :-
    core_cell(RC,CC,V),
    dr = RC - core_row_min,
    dc = CC - core_col_min,
    input(R0 + dr, C0 + dc, V), V != 0.

% Zero anchor candidate (zero cell, fits, no conflict, not original)
zero_anchor_candidate(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 >= core_row_min,
    R0 + core_h - 1 <= max_row,
    C0 >= core_col_min,
    C0 + core_w - 1 <= max_col,
    not conflict(R0,C0),
    (R0,C0) != (core_row_min, core_col_min).

% Core cells: non-zero with at least one orthogonal neighbor with non-zero.
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :- neighbor(R,C,R2,C2), input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0.

core_cell(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

% Core rows/cols
row_core(R) :- core_cell(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core_cell(_,C,_).

% Bounding box of core cells
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Tile size
core_h = core_row_max - core_row_min + 1.
core_w = core_col_max - core_col_min + 1.

% Max indices
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Core tile values (offset -> value)
core_tile_value(DR,DC,V) :- core_cell(RC,CC,V), RC = core_row_min + DR, CC = core_col_min + DC.

% Conflict predicate: placing tile at (R0,C0) would overlap a non-zero core cell.
conflict(R0,C0) :-
    core_tile_value(DR,DC,V),
    R0 + DR = RC2,
    C0 + DC = CC2,
    input(RC2,CC2,V), V != 0.

Note: core_tile_value(DR,DC,V) also binds DR,DC,V and also uses core_row_min, core_col_min; we also need RC2,CC2 bound.

% Zero anchor candidate (zero cell, fits, no conflict)
zero_anchor_candidate(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 >= core_row_min,
    R0 + core_h - 1 <= max_row,
    C0 >= core_col_min,
    C0 + core_w - 1 <= max_col,
    not conflict(R0,C0).

% We enforce exactly one zero anchor selected (choose the lexicographically maximal)
% first define candidate set
candidate(R,C) :- zero_anchor_candidate(R,C).

% Exactly one selected (choice)
% We'll use a simple choice: pick any candidate; to make deterministic we can enforce ordering:
% For any two candidates, we cannot have both true if the second is lexicographically larger.

% However to keep program short, we can use a built-in "choose" with ordering:
% We can define selected(R0,C0) :- zero_anchor_candidate(R0,C0),

% Then zero_anchor(R0,C0) :- zero_anchor_candidate(R0,C0), not exists_candidate(R0,C0,R1,C1).

% To ensure exactly one zero_anchor, we can also enforce exactly one selected (the above already ensures at most one, but if there is no candidate? there will be at least one). We'll also have core anchor copy always.

% Core copy (original position)
core_copy(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    core_tile_value(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = core_row_min + DR,

% Zero anchor copy (selected)

% Output cell definitions

% Cells not covered by any copy must be zero.

% Exactly one color per cell

% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.

% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.

<asp>
% Core non‑zero cells that have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R + DR, C2 = C + DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C+1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C-1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+1, C, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R-1, C, V), V != 0).

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows and columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows and columns (size of the tile)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% 0‑based index of a core row / column
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Sub‑grid cell (includes zeros – we keep the whole rectangle)
subcell(R,C,V) :- row_core(R), col_core(C), input(R,C,V).

% Output cell – map each tile (BR,BC) and each sub‑grid cell (DR,DC) to an absolute position
output_cell(R_abs, C_abs) :-
    core(BR, BC, _),               % anchor cell of the tile
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),           % value of the copied cell
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one colour for every output cell
1 { output(R_abs, C_abs, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs, C_abs).

% Non‑zero anchor tile – copy the whole sub‑grid into the tile
:- core(BR, BC, V_anchor), V_anchor != 0,
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, V2).

% Zero anchor tile – the whole tile must be all zero
:- core(BR, BC, 0),
    subcell(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, 0).

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Offsets for 4‑connected neighbourhood
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% A cell has at least one orthogonal neighbour with a non‑zero value
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- neighbor(R,C,R2,C2),
                            input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% Core cells: non‑zero and have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows / columns that contain at least one core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows / columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% Index of a core row / column (0‑based)
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Sub‑grid cell – the values of the core cells (the “tile”)
subcell(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V).

% Position of a copied cell (tile placement)
output_cell(R_abs,C_abs) :-
    core(BR,BC,_),               % anchor cell of the tile

    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR,I), col_index_of(BC,J),
    row_index_of(DR,I2), col_index_of(DC,J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one colour for every output cell
1 { output(R_abs,C_abs,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs,C_abs).
</asp>
