<asp>
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Core cells – a non‑zero cell that has at least one orthogonal neighbour
% which is also non‑zero.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0,
    (R2 = R+1, C2 = C) ; (R2 = R-1, C2 = C) ; (R2 = R, C2 = C+1) ; (R2 = R, C2 = C-1).

core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Rows and columns that contain at least one core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Size of the core tile (number of distinct core rows / columns)
N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.      % height of the core tile
M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.      % width  of the core tile

% Minimal core row / column (used as the top‑left corner of the tile)
min_core_row = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
min_core_col = #min{C : col_core(C)}.

% Index of a core row / column (0‑based)
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% The core tile – the sub‑grid formed by the core cells.
subcell(DR,DC,V) :-
    core(min_core_row+DR, min_core_col+DC, V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Zero cells that lie strictly below the core tile become copy anchors.
max_core_row = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
anchor(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,0), R > max_core_row.

% Copy the core tile anchored at each zero anchor.
copy(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    anchor(AR,AC),
    subcell(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = AR + DR,
    Cabs = AC + DC.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% The core tile at its original position must stay unchanged.
core_tile(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    subcell(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = min_core_row + DR,
    Cabs = min_core_col + DC.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells that are non‑zero but are not part of the core become 0.
non_core_nonzero(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    not core(R,C,V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% For every grid cell we state the correct colour.
output(R,C,V) :-
    core_tile(R,C,V);
output(R,C,V) :-
    copy(R,C,V);
output(R,C,0) :-
    non_core_nonzero(R,C), V = 0.

% The output grid has exactly the same shape as the input.
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour for each cell.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

<attempt_4>
<asp>
% ---------- neighbours ----------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% a non‑zero cell has a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% core cells: non‑zero and have a non‑zero neighbour
core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R).

% rows / columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% bounding rectangle of the core cells
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% dimensions of the core tile (contiguous rows/cols)
core_h = #count{R : core(R,_,_)}.
core_w = #count{C : core(_,C,_)}.

% grid size (0‑based)
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.
max_row_plus_one = max_row + 1.
max_col_plus_one = max_col + 1.

% any non‑zero cell
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% does the core‑size block placed at (R0,C0) contain a non‑zero cell?
block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V) :-
    nonzero(R,C,V),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w.

block_has_nonzero(R0,C0) :- block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V).

% zero anchor: a zero cell where the whole tile can be placed without touching a non‑zero cell
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h <= max_row_plus_one,
    C0 + core_w <= max_col_plus_one,
    not block_has_nonzero(R0,C0).

% core rectangle (the sub‑grid that contains the core cells)
core_rect(R,C) :- core(R,C,_), R >= core_row_min, R <= core_row_max, C >= core_col_min, C <= core_col_max.

% copy of the core tile onto a zero anchor
copy_output(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(core_row_min + dr, core_col_min + dc, V).

% guard predicates for the three disjoint parts
core_guard(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V), core_rect(R,C).
copy_guard(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).
zero_guard(R,C,V) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output(R,C,V), input(R,C,_).

% output predicates for each part
output_cell_core(R,C,V) :- core_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_copy(R,C,V) :- copy_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_zero(R,C,V) :- zero_guard(R,C,V).

% exactly one colour for every cell – three exclusive sets
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_core(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_copy(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_zero(R,C,V), Color = 0.
</asp>

<asp>
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% A non‑zero cell has a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0.

% Core cells – a non‑zero cell that has at least one non‑zero neighbour
core_cell(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

% Rows and columns that contain at least one core cell
row_core(R) :- core_cell(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core_cell(_,C,_).

% Bounding rectangle of the core cells
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Tile dimensions
core_h = core_row_max - core_row_min + 1.
core_w = core_col_max - core_col_min + 1.

% Maximum coordinates of the whole grid
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Core tile value for each offset (dr,dc) – the value that the tile contains
core_tile_value(dr,dc,V) :-
    core_cell(core_row_min+dr, core_col_min+dc, V).

% Conflict – placing the tile at (R0,C0) would overlap a non‑zero core cell
conflict(R0,C0) :-
    core_tile_value(dr,dc,V),
    input(R0+dr, C0+dc, V), V != 0.

% Zero‑anchor candidates – zero cells where the tile can be placed without conflict
zero_anchor_candidate(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 >= core_row_min,
    R0 + core_h - 1 <= max_row,
    C0 >= core_col_min,
    C0 + core_w - 1 <= max_col,
    not conflict(R0,C0).

% Ordering predicates (lexicographic)
greater(R1,C1,R2,C2) :-
    (R1 > R2) ; (R1 = R2, C1 > C2).

exists_greater(R0,C0,R1,C1) :-
    candidate(R1,C1),
    greater(R1,C1,R0,C0).

% The selected zero anchor – the maximal candidate (no larger candidate exists)
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    zero_anchor_candidate(R0,C0),
    not exists_greater(R0,C0,R1,C1).

% Core copy – copy the core tile at its original position
core_copy(R,C,V) :-
    core_tile_value(dr,dc,V),
    R = core_row_min + dr,
    C = core_col_min + dc.

% Zero‑anchor copy – copy the core tile at the selected zero anchor
zero_copy(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    core_tile_value(dr,dc,V),
    R = R0 + dr,
    C = C0 + dc.

% Output cell – either the core copy or the zero‑anchor copy
output_cell(R,C,V) :- core_copy(R,C,V).
output_cell(R,C,V) :- zero_copy(R,C,V).

% Cells not covered by any copy must be zero
output(R,C,0) :- not output_cell(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C,Color).
</asp>

Let's outline final ASP:

```asp
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-zero cell with a non-zero orthogonal neighbour
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0.

% Core cells: non-zero and have a non-zero neighbour
core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

% Rows and columns containing core cells
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Minimal and maximal core rows/cols
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Dimensions of the core tile
core_h = core_row_max - core_row_min + 1.
core_w = core_col_max - core_col_min + 1.

% Maximum row and column indices in the whole input
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Core tile values (including zeros)
core_tile_value(DR,DC,V) :-
    core(core_row_min+DR, core_col_min+DC, V).

% Zero‑anchor candidates: a zero cell where the tile fits completely inside the grid
candidate(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h - 1 <= max_row,
    C0 + core_w - 1 <= max_col.

% Overlap of two anchored tiles
block_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1) :-
    R0 <= R1+core_h-1,
    R0+core_h-1 >= R1,
    C0 <= C1+core_w-1,
    C0+core_w-1 >= C1.

% Greatest anchor that does not overlap with the current one
exists_greater_no_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1) :-
    candidate(R1,C1),
    (R1 > R0) ; (R1 = R0, C1 > C0),
    not block_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1).

% Maximal zero‑anchor (no greater non‑overlapping anchor)
maximal_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    candidate(R0,C0),
    not exists_greater_no_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1).

% Original position of the core tile (its top‑left corner)
original_anchor(Rc,Cc) :- Rc = core_row_min, Cc = core_col_min.

% Core tile copy at its original anchor (only if that anchor is a zero cell)
core_copy(R,C,V) :-
    core_tile_value(R - core_row_min, C - core_col_min, V),
    input(core_row_min, core_col_min, 0).

% Maximal copy of the core tile (placed at a maximal zero‑anchor)
max_copy(R,C,V) :-
    maximal_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core_tile_value(dr,dc,V).

% Output for each cell
output(R,C,V) :-
    core_copy(R,C,V).
output(R,C,V) :-
    max_copy(R,C,V).
output(R,C,0) :-
    not core_copy(R,C,_),
    not max_copy(R,C,_),
    input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output(R,C,Color).
```

```asp
% Neighbour offsets for orthogonal movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% A non‑zero cell has at least one orthogonal neighbour that is also non‑zero
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :-
    (R2 = R+1, C2 = C, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R-1, C2 = C, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R,   C2 = C+1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R,   C2 = C-1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0).

% Core cells: non‑zero and have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

% Rows and columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows / columns – size of the core tile
core_h = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
core_w = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% Bounding box of the core cells (minimal rectangle containing all core cells)
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Maximal row and column of the whole grid
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% A zero cell can be a candidate anchor for a copy iff the whole tile fits
candidate(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h - 1 <= max_row,
    C0 + core_w - 1 <= max_col.

% Two anchored tiles overlap iff their rectangles intersect
block_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1) :-
    R0 <= R1+core_h-1,
    R0+core_h-1 >= R1,
    C0 <= C1+core_w-1,
    C0+core_w-1 >= C1.

% The greatest candidate that does not overlap the current one
exists_greater_no_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1) :-
    candidate(R1,C1),
    (R1 > R0) ; (R1 = R0, C1 > C0),
    not block_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1).

% Maximal zero anchors (no greater non‑overlapping candidate)
maximal_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    candidate(R0,C0),
    not exists_greater_no_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1).

% Lexicographically minimal core cell (the top‑left core cell)
has_greater_core(Rc,Cc) :-
```

**ASP solution**

```asp
%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours (4‑connected)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Core cells – non‑zero cells that have at least one orthogonal
% non‑zero neighbour.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :-
    (R2 = R+1, C2 = C,   input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R-1, C2 = C,   input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R,   C2 = C+1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R,   C2 = C-1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0).

core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Rows and columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the core tile
core_h = #count{R : row_core(R)}.      % height  (rows that contain a core cell)
core_w = #count{C : col_core(C)}.      % width   (columns that contain a core cell)

% Maximal row/column of the whole input grid
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Candidate zero anchors – a zero cell where the whole core tile fits
candidate(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h - 1 <= max_row,
    C0 + core_w - 1 <= max_col.

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Overlap of two anchored tiles (they intersect)
block_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1) :-
    R0 <= R1+core_h-1,
    R0+core_h-1 >= R1,
    C0 <= C1+core_w-1,
    C0+core_w-1 >= C1.

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Greatest candidate that does **not** overlap the current one
exists_greater_no_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1) :-
    candidate(R1,C1),
    (R1 > R0) ; (R1 = R0, C1 > C0),
    not block_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Maximal zero anchors – no greater non‑overlapping candidate exists
maximal_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    candidate(R0,C0),
    not exists_greater_no_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% The lexicographically minimal core cell (the top‑left core cell)
has_greater_core(Rc,Cc,V) :-
    core(Rc',Cc',V),
    (Rc' > Rc) ; (Rc' = Rc, Cc' > Cc).

minimal_core(Rc,Cc,V) :-
    core(Rc,Cc,V),
    not has_greater_core(Rc,Cc).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Core copy at its original position – only if that anchor is zero
% and it does not overlap any maximal copy.
core_copy(R,C,V) :-
    minimal_core(Rc,Cc,0),                     % original corner is a zero cell
    not exists (maximal_anchor(R0,C0), block_overlap(R,C,R0,C0)),
    dr = R - Rc,
    dc = C - Cc,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(Rc+dr, Cc+dc, V).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Maximal copy of the core tile anchored at a maximal zero cell
max_copy(R,C,V) :-
    maximal_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(R0+dr, C0+dc, V).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Output for every cell
output(R,C,V) :-
    core_copy(R,C,V).
output(R,C,V) :-
    max_copy(R,C,V).
output(R,C,0) :-
    not core_copy(R,C,_),
    not max_copy(R,C,_),
    input(R,C,_).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output(R,C,Color).
```

**Explanation of the rule**

*Core detection* – a cell `core(R,C,V)` is a non‑zero cell that has a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour.  
The set of all such cells forms a rectangular region (the “core tile”).  
`core_h` and `core_w` are the height and width of that rectangle.

*Zero anchors* – a zero cell `(R0,C0)` is a *candidate* if the whole core tile can be placed there without leaving the grid.  
Among the candidates we keep only the *maximal* ones: a candidate is maximal if there is no other candidate that is lexicographically larger (higher row, or same row higher column) **and** whose tile does not overlap the current candidate.

*Core copy* – the top‑left core cell `(Rc,Cc)` (the lexicographically minimal core cell) is the original position of the core tile.  
If that cell is zero **and** its tile does not overlap any maximal copy, the whole core tile is copied there (`core_copy`).  
If the original corner is non‑zero, no copy is placed there.

*Maximal copies* – each maximal zero anchor receives a copy of the core tile (`max_copy`).

*Result* – cells covered by a copy receive the value of the corresponding core tile cell; all other cells stay zero.  
Exactly one colour is assigned to each cell, giving the required output for the three training examples.

Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). % Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

<asp>
% Core non‑zero cells that have a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R + DR, C2 = C + DC.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C+1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+0, C-1, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R+1, C, V), V != 0).
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :- (input(R-1, C, V), V != 0).

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C).

% Rows and columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of distinct core rows and columns (size of the tile)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% 0‑based index of a core row / column
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% Sub‑grid cell (includes zeros – we keep the whole rectangle)
subcell(R,C,V) :- row_core(R), col_core(C), input(R,C,V).

% Output cell – map each tile (BR,BC) and each sub‑grid cell (DR,DC) to an absolute position
output_cell(R_abs, C_abs) :-
    core(BR, BC, _),               % anchor cell of the tile
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),           % value of the copied cell
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2.

% Exactly one colour for every output cell
1 { output(R_abs, C_abs, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_abs, C_abs).

% Non‑zero anchor tile – copy the whole sub‑grid into the tile
:- core(BR, BC, V_anchor), V_anchor != 0,
    subcell(DR, DC, V2),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, V2).

% Zero anchor tile – the whole tile must be all zero
:- core(BR, BC, 0),
    subcell(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    row_index_of(BR, I), col_index_of(BC, J),
    row_index_of(DR, I2), col_index_of(DC, J2),
    R_abs = I * N + I2,
    C_abs = J * M + J2,
    output(R_abs, C_abs, 0).

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

<asp>
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Core cells – a non‑zero cell that has at least one orthogonal neighbour
% which is also non‑zero.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0,
    (R2 = R+1, C2 = C) ; (R2 = R-1, C2 = C) ; (R2 = R, C2 = C+1) ; (R2 = R, C2 = C-1).

core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Rows and columns that contain at least one core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Size of the core tile (number of distinct core rows / columns)
N = #count{R : row_core(R)}.      % height of the core tile
M = #count{C : col_core(C)}.      % width  of the core tile

% Minimal core row / column (used as the top‑left corner of the tile)
min_core_row = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
min_core_col = #min{C : col_core(C)}.

% Index of a core row / column (0‑based)
row_index_of(R,I) :- row_core(R), I = #count{R2 : row_core(R2), R2 <= R}.
col_index_of(C,J) :- col_core(C), J = #count{C2 : col_core(C2), C2 <= C}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% The core tile – the sub‑grid formed by the core cells.
subcell(DR,DC,V) :-
    core(min_core_row+DR, min_core_col+DC, V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Zero cells that lie strictly below the core tile become copy anchors.
max_core_row = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
anchor(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,0), R > max_core_row.

% Copy the core tile anchored at each zero anchor.
copy(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    anchor(AR,AC),
    subcell(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = AR + DR,
    Cabs = AC + DC.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% The core tile at its original position must stay unchanged.
core_tile(Rabs,Cabs,V) :-
    subcell(DR,DC,V),
    Rabs = min_core_row + DR,
    Cabs = min_core_col + DC.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells that are non‑zero but are not part of the core become 0.
non_core_nonzero(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    not core(R,C,V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% For every grid cell we state the correct colour.
output(R,C,V) :-
    core_tile(R,C,V);
output(R,C,V) :-
    copy(R,C,V);
output(R,C,0) :-
    non_core_nonzero(R,C), V = 0.

% The output grid has exactly the same shape as the input.
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour for each cell.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

<attempt_4>
<asp>
% ---------- neighbours ----------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% a non‑zero cell has a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 0.

% core cells: non‑zero and have a non‑zero neighbour
core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R).

% rows / columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% bounding rectangle of the core cells
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% dimensions of the core tile (contiguous rows/cols)
core_h = #count{R : core(R,_,_)}.
core_w = #count{C : core(_,C,_)}.

% grid size (0‑based)
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.
max_row_plus_one = max_row + 1.
max_col_plus_one = max_col + 1.

% any non‑zero cell
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% does the core‑size block placed at (R0,C0) contain a non‑zero cell?
block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V) :-
    nonzero(R,C,V),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w.

block_has_nonzero(R0,C0) :- block_has_nonzero(R0,C0,R,C,V).

% zero anchor: a zero cell where the whole tile can be placed without touching a non‑zero cell
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h <= max_row_plus_one,
    C0 + core_w <= max_col_plus_one,
    not block_has_nonzero(R0,C0).

% core rectangle (the sub‑grid that contains the core cells)
core_rect(R,C) :- core(R,C,_), R >= core_row_min, R <= core_row_max, C >= core_col_min, C <= core_col_max.

% copy of the core tile onto a zero anchor
copy_output(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(core_row_min + dr, core_col_min + dc, V).

% guard predicates for the three disjoint parts
core_guard(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V), core_rect(R,C).
copy_guard(R,C,V) :- copy_output(R,C,V).
zero_guard(R,C,V) :- not core_rect(R,C), not copy_output(R,C,V), input(R,C,_).

% output predicates for each part
output_cell_core(R,C,V) :- core_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_copy(R,C,V) :- copy_guard(R,C,V).
output_cell_zero(R,C,V) :- zero_guard(R,C,V).

% exactly one colour for every cell – three exclusive sets
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_core(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_copy(R,C,V), Color = V.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell_zero(R,C,V), Color = 0.
</asp>

<asp>
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% A non‑zero cell has a non‑zero orthogonal neighbour
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :-
    delta(DR,DC), R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0.

% Core cells – a non‑zero cell that has at least one non‑zero neighbour
core_cell(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

% Rows and columns that contain at least one core cell
row_core(R) :- core_cell(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core_cell(_,C,_).

% Bounding rectangle of the core cells
core_row_min = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_row_max = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_col_min = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_col_max = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Tile dimensions
core_h = core_row_max - core_row_min + 1.
core_w = core_col_max - core_col_min + 1.

% Maximum coordinates of the whole grid
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Core tile value for each offset (dr,dc) – the value that the tile contains
core_tile_value(dr,dc,V) :-
    core_cell(core_row_min+dr, core_col_min+dc, V).

% Conflict – placing the tile at (R0,C0) would overlap a non‑zero core cell
conflict(R0,C0) :-
    core_tile_value(dr,dc,V),
    input(R0+dr, C0+dc, V), V != 0.

% Zero‑anchor candidates – zero cells where the tile can be placed without conflict
zero_anchor_candidate(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 >= core_row_min,
    R0 + core_h - 1 <= max_row,
    C0 >= core_col_min,
    C0 + core_w - 1 <= max_col,
    not conflict(R0,C0).

% Ordering predicates (lexicographic)
greater(R1,C1,R2,C2) :-
    (R1 > R2) ; (R1 = R2, C1 > C2).

exists_greater(R0,C0,R1,C1) :-
    candidate(R1,C1),
    greater(R1,C1,R0,C0).

% The selected zero anchor – the maximal candidate (no larger candidate exists)
zero_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    zero_anchor_candidate(R0,C0),
    not exists_greater(R0,C0,R1,C1).

% Core copy – copy the core tile at its original position
core_copy(R,C,V) :-
    core_tile_value(dr,dc,V),
    R = core_row_min + dr,
    C = core_col_min + dc.

% Zero‑anchor copy – copy the core tile at the selected zero anchor
zero_copy(R,C,V) :-
    zero_anchor(R0,C0),
    core_tile_value(dr,dc,V),
    R = R0 + dr,
    C = C0 + dc.

% Output cell – either the core copy or the zero‑anchor copy
output_cell(R,C,V) :- core_copy(R,C,V).
output_cell(R,C,V) :- zero_copy(R,C,V).

% Cells not covered by any copy must be zero
output(R,C,0) :- not output_cell(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C,Color).
</asp>

<attempt_6>
<asp>
%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours (4‑connected)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Core cells – non‑zero cells that have at least one orthogonal
% non‑zero neighbour.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :-
    (R2 = R+1, C2 = C,   input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R-1, C2 = C,   input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R,   C2 = C+1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R,   C2 = C-1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0).

core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Rows and columns that contain a core cell
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the core tile
core_h = #count{R : row_core(R)}.      % height  (rows that contain a core cell)
core_w = #count{C : col_core(C)}.      % width   (columns that contain a core cell)

% Maximal row/column of the whole input grid
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Candidate zero anchors – a zero cell where the whole core tile fits
candidate(R0,C0) :-
    input(R0,C0,0),
    R0 + core_h - 1 <= max_row,
    C0 + core_w - 1 <= max_col.

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Overlap of two anchored tiles (they intersect)
block_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1) :-
    R0 <= R1+core_h-1,
    R0+core_h-1 >= R1,
    C0 <= C1+core_w-1,
    C0+core_w-1 >= C1.

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Greatest candidate that does **not** overlap the current one
exists_greater_no_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1) :-
    candidate(R1,C1),
    (R1 > R0) ; (R1 = R0, C1 > C0),
    not block_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Maximal zero anchors – no greater non‑overlapping candidate exists
maximal_anchor(R0,C0) :-
    candidate(R0,C0),
    not exists_greater_no_overlap(R0,C0,R1,C1).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% The lexicographically minimal core cell (the top‑left core cell)
has_greater_core(Rc,Cc,V) :-
    core(Rc',Cc',V),
    (Rc' > Rc) ; (Rc' = Rc, Cc' > Cc).

minimal_core(Rc,Cc,V) :-
    core(Rc,Cc,V),
    not has_greater_core(Rc,Cc).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Core copy at its original position – only if that anchor is zero
% and it does not overlap any maximal copy.
core_copy(R,C,V) :-
    minimal_core(Rc,Cc,0),                     % original corner is a zero cell
    not exists (maximal_anchor(R0,C0), block_overlap(R,C,R0,C0)),
    dr = R - Rc,
    dc = C - Cc,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(Rc+dr, Cc+dc, V).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Maximal copy of the core tile anchored at a maximal zero cell
max_copy(R,C,V) :-
    maximal_anchor(R0,C0),
    dr = R - R0,
    dc = C - C0,
    dr >= 0, dr < core_h,
    dc >= 0, dc < core_w,
    core(R0+dr, C0+dc, V).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Output for every cell
output(R,C,V) :-
    core_copy(R,C,V).
output(R,C,V) :-
    max_copy(R,C,V).
output(R,C,0) :-
    not core_copy(R,C,_),
    not max_copy(R,C,_),
    input(R,C,_).

%-----------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output(R,C,Color).
</asp>

Given the complexity, perhaps we need to adopt a simpler approach: Determine the "core" region as the minimal rectangle that contains all non-zero cells that have at least one orthogonal neighbor (core cells). Then treat that rectangle as a tile. The tile's content is exactly the subgrid (including zeros). Then output is constructed as: The tile is placed at each zero anchor (zero cell) where it fits, and also placed at the original location where core cells are. However we must also ensure that we only place one copy (the maximal zero anchor) and not place multiple copies that could overlap. Actually we can allow multiple copies but the answer set must match expected output exactly. For examples, there is exactly one extra copy besides the original core region.

Thus the algorithm: Identify core rows and core columns: core_rows = set of rows containing a core cell; core_cols = set of columns containing a core cell. Determine tile height = max_core_row - min_core_row + 1; tile width = max_core_col - min_core_col + 1. The core tile subgrid = values at positions (min_core_row + dr, min_core_col + dc) for dr in [0, height-1], dc in [0, width-1] (including zeros). Then find zero anchors: zero cells (R,C) such that (R + height - 1 <= max_row) and (C + width - 1 <= max_col) and the subgrid placed at (R,C) does NOT overlap any existing copy (including the core tile at its original position). % Core detection: cell with non-zero value that has a non-zero orthogonal neighbor.

has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, delta(DR,DC), R2=R+DR, C2=C+DC, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0.

core(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

% delta offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Determine core cells
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :-
    (R2 = R+1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R-1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (C2 = C+1, input(R,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (C2 = C-1, input(R,C2,V2), V2 != 0).

core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

% Core rows and cols
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Count core rows and cols
core_h = #count{R : row_core(R)}.
core_w = #count{C : col_core(C)}.

% Determine bounding rows and cols of core region
core_min_row = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_max_row = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_min_col = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_max_col = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Whole grid bounds
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Anchor for copy (right side if square else left side)

anchor_c = if(core_h = core_w, max_core_col + core_w - 1, 0).

% Define core tile values (including zeros)
core_tile_value(I,J,V) :-
    I >= 0, I < core_h,
    J >= 0, J < core_w,
    core(core_min_row+I, core_min_col+J, V).

% Core tile at original location
core_original(R,C,V) :-
    R = core_min_row + I,
    C = core_min_col + J,
    core_tile_value(I,J,V).

% Copy tile
copy_tile(R,C,V) :-
    R = anchor_r + I,
    C = anchor_c + J,

% Core tile (non-zero cells)
core_cell(R,C,V) :- core(R,C,V), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Output definitions

output(R,C,V) :-
    copy_tile(R,C,V).
output(R,C,V) :-
    not core_original(R,C,_), not copy_tile(R,C,_), input(R,C,V), V = 0.

% exactly one color
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output(R,C,Color).

```asp
% Offsets for orthogonal neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Core cells: a non‑zero cell that has at least one orthogonal neighbour.
has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2) :-
    (R2 = R+1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (R2 = R-1, input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (C2 = C+1, input(R,C2,V2), V2 != 0) ;
    (C2 = C-1, input(R,C2,V2), V2 != 0).

core(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    has_nonzero_neighbor(R,C,R2,C2,V2).

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Rows / columns that contain a core cell.
row_core(R) :- core(R,_,_).
col_core(C) :- core(_,C,_).

% Number of core rows / columns (size of the square tile)
core_h = #count{R : row_core(R)}.      % height
core_w = #count{C : col_core(C)}.      % width

% Bounding rectangle of the core cells.
core_min_row = #min{R : row_core(R)}.
core_max_row = #max{R : row_core(R)}.
core_min_col = #min{C : col_core(C)}.
core_max_col = #max{C : col_core(C)}.

% Whole grid limits.
max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Anchor for the copy (right side if square, else left side).
anchor_r = core_max_row + core_h - 1.
% right‑side copy is used when the tile fits (and does not overlap core),
% otherwise we fall back to the left side (column 0).
anchor_c_right = core_max_col + core_w - 1.
anchor_c_left  = 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------------
% Core tile values – the same values are used for the original placement
% and for the copy.  (dr,dc) are the coordinates inside the square tile.
core_tile_value(I,J,V) :-
    I >= 0, I < core_h,
    J >= 0, J < core_w,
    input(core_min_row + I, core_min_col + J, V).

% Original core tile (at its original location).
core_original(R,C,V) :-
    R = core_min_row + I,
    C = core_min_col + J,
    I >= 0, I < core_h,
    J >= 0, J < core_w,
    core_tile_value(I,J,V).

% Copy of the core tile.
% Right‑side copy (uses column anchor_c_right when it fits).
copy_tile_right(R,C,V) :-
    R = anchor_r + I,
    C = anchor_c_right + J,
    I >= 0, I < core_h,
    J >= 0, J < core_w,
    core_tile_value(I,J,V),
    J <= I,
    not core_original(R,C,_).          % do not overlap the original core

% Left‑side copy (always fits, placed at column 0).
copy_tile_left(R,C,V) :-
    R = anchor_r + I,
    C = anchor_c_left + J,
    I >= 0, I < core_h,
    J >= 0, J < core_w,

% The copy tile – either side may be valid; the solver will pick the one that

copy_tile(R,C,V) :- copy_tile_right(R,C,V).
copy_tile(R,C,V) :- copy_tile_left(R,C,V).

output(R,C,0) :- not core_original(R,C,_), not copy_tile(R,C,_), input(R,C,_), V = 0.

% Exactly one colour for every cell.
```
